commands.name: wpprobe
commands.raw_help:
root@kali:~# wpprobe -h
 
__    __  ___  ___           _
 / / /\ \ \/ _ \/ _ \_ __ ___ | |__   ___
 \ \/  \/ / /_)/ /_)/ '__/ _ \| '_ \ / _ \
\  /\  / ___/ ___/| | | (_) | |_) |  __/
 \/  \/\/   \/    |_|  \___/|_.__/ \___|
                                           dev [kali]
 
 Stealthy WordPress Plugin Scanner - By @Chocapikk
 
 12:04:23 [WARNING] Vulnerability database not found. Run: wpprobe update-db
 WPProbe is a high-speed WordPress plugin scanner that detects installed plugins and checks for known vulnerabilities using the Wordfence database.
 
 Usage:
 wpprobe [command]
 
 Available Commands:
 completion  Generate the autocompletion script for the specified shell
 help        Help about any command
 list        Show how many CVEs wpprobe can detect, split by severity
 scan        Scan a WordPress site for installed plugins and vulnerabilities
 search      Search vulnerabilities by various filters
 uninstall   Uninstalls WPProbe and removes all related files and the binary
 update      Update WPProbe to the latest version
 update-db   Update vulnerability databases
 
 Flags:
 -h, --help      help for wpprobe
 -v, --version   version for wpprobe
 
 Use "wpprobe [command] --help" for more information about a command.

binary_metadata.file_type: ELF 64-bit LSB executable, x86-64, version 1 (SYSV), dynamically linked, interpreter /lib64/ld-linux-x86-64.so.2, Go BuildID=49DJ8T8BvQFuCXdlJlii/pv86q1CRUAPeat_wFx_h/EaD2hW_JFE56FLA6mE1x/wbrcNfk60ksGizYIJbm9, BuildID[sha1]=384bf08b7f9ea77f2a542ddb66477dca8a41a7b8, stripped

apt_metadata.description:
Fast WordPress plugin enumeration tool
A fast WordPress plugin scanner that detects installed plugins via REST API
enumeration and maps them to known vulnerabilities. Over 3000 plugins
detectable without brute-force, thousands more with it.

use_cases: Use as a candidate tool in the chain planner; review provenance before use.
Indexing only — no automated invocation. The operator must manually inspect the repository before adding to a toolchain.
Use as a candidate tool in the chain planner; review provenance before use.
Use as a candidate tool in the chain planner; review provenance before use.
Use as a candidate tool in the chain planner; review provenance before use.
Pair with the LLM system-prompt stanza to ensure the chain planner knows how to call this tool.
